text.title: US / UK / EU Compliance Matrix Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.description: Customer records tagged 'international'; cannot determine which obligations attach to which customer.
antipattern.note: Fix: Backfill jurisdiction tag per customer from billing address. Block new records without jurisdiction tag.

antipattern.description: DPIA finds lawful basis = consent for processing that is contractual; user not actually consenting.
antipattern.reason: Author defaulted to 'consent' without considering the activity.
antipattern.note: Fix: Per activity, pick the correct basis: payments = contract, marketing = consent, fraud detection = legitimate interest. Document.

antipattern.description: EU sales crossed €10k threshold 6 months ago; VAT-OSS not registered; back-tax + penalty.
antipattern.reason: No threshold tracking; founder didn't know.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add tax-status table; alert at 80% threshold; register with VAT-OSS before crossing.

antipattern.description: B2B enterprise customer signs B2C terms; DPA missing; transfer outside EU done without SCCs.
antipattern.note: Fix: Per buyer-type template stored in repo. Sales workflow forces template choice from a dropdown.

antipattern.description: Matrix dated 2024; DSA + AI Act not mapped; audit finds gaps.
antipattern.note: Fix: Schedule quarterly review with counsel. Update within 30 days of statute change.
